claim_a_context_portability.wording: Semantic context beat size-matched random candidate selection on truth inclusion and exact recovery for MAP and official IBM BCF in the tested single-product cells.

claim_b_factorizer_internal_semantic_ben.wording: A substrate-internal semantic benefit beyond truth retention was not consistently demonstrated across MAP and BCF.

claim_c_map_stronger_than_bcf.wording: MAP was stronger than the audited official IBM BCF implementation in the tested operating envelope.

claim_d_external_context_transfer.wording: The external context-selection mechanism transferred across two representation/factorizer families.

allowed_claims: Semantic context beats random candidate selection.
The external context-selection mechanism transferred from MAP to official IBM BCF.
MAP was stronger than official IBM BCF in the tested single-product operating envelope.
forbidden_claims: Verifier, fallback policy, or open-world lifecycle already transferred to BCF.
MAP is generally superior to all block-code factorizers.
